prevalence.source: WHO Global TB Report 2024 / NTEP (National TB Elimination Programme)
prevalence.trend: slowly decreasing notifications; ongoing underdiagnosis and high transmission in dense urban settings

burden.source: IHME Global Burden of Disease (GBD) / WHO

riskFactors: silica exposure (e.g., mining) and indoor air pollution
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Sputum NAAT (Xpert/Truenat), smear microscopy, culture/DST where available; CXR as triage; NTEP diagnostic algorithms

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: active case finding in high-risk groups; variable across states; symptom screening common in primary care
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: ~60-70% treatment success; gaps for MDR-TB and private-sector patients

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: NTEP algorithms with Truenat/Xpert scale-up; CXR camps for screening in high-burden districts
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Free anti-TB drugs via NTEP (daily fixed-dose combinations); MDR/RR-TB regimens (including bedaquiline-based where indicated) in DR-TB centers; linkage with HIV program for TB-HIV
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Large private-sector care with variable reporting, MDR/RR-TB burden, delayed presentation, and high catastrophic costs despite free drugs
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: National NTEP coverage with expanding rapid molecular testing and digital adherence support, and targeted active case-finding campaigns
